summary: notify-theme fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com while impersonating pino
details: notify-theme@1.3.5 is a pino-logger impersonation package that loads and executes attacker-controlled JavaScript. index.js exports a middleware factory that, on first invocation, spawns a detached `node lib/caller.js` child process with stdio ignored and unref'd. lib/caller.js issues an axios GET to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/EXSIF (with a base64-encoded backup URL https://jsonkeeper.com/b/ZK45J stored in lib/const.js), reads the `cookie` field from the response, and executes it as arbitrary JavaScript via `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)`, passing the installer's `require` into the fetched code. A 5x retry loop makes delivery reliable. The package name is unrelated to logging, but README badges point at npm's pino, module.exports exposes a `pino` alias, and lib/ mirrors pino's file layout (proto.js, levels.js, redaction.js, transport.js, symbols.js, worker.js) to trick developers into installing it as a drop-in logger. Once the middleware is invoked, whoever controls the jsonkeeper.com paste has full remote code execution in the consumer's Node.js process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
